_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize statewide-exec batch: Rokita (IN AG), Kobach (KS AG), Beshear (KY Gov), Kelly (KS Gov), Toland (KS LtGov). All state tier. Cited; cross-party deviations credited. Toland candidacy corrected to not_running (announced 2025-07-22).

todd-rokita.notes_set: Indiana AG (R), re-elected 2024 (term to Jan 2029); former US Rep IN-04 + IN Sec of State. Uniformly conservative record, no cross-party deviations found (a 2023 IN Supreme Court attorney-conduct reprimand over the Caitlin Bernard case is a professional-conduct matter, not a policy deviation). Documented: personhood/Life-at-Conception co-sponsor + defended near-total ban; CIDs to gender clinics + 22-state coalition vs gender procedures for minors; Parents' Bill of Rights (school choice, pronoun-notification, anti-CRT 'Eyes on Education', religious-liberty/prayer section); sued porn sites under age-verification law + 24-state SCOTUS brief; led antitrust/ESG suits vs BlackRock/State Street/Vanguard; won 7th Cir stay on student-ID voting + DHS/USCIS citizenship-verification settlement; prolific anti-Biden federal-overreach suits; sued sanctuary jurisdictions under IN's ban; NRA A-rated. Null where no evidence (IVF/euthanasia, CBDC, hand-count/mail-in, cashless bail, NFA/castle).

0.note: Co-sponsored Sanctity of Human Life Act + Life at Conception Act (personhood from fertilization).

1.note: Personhood-at-conception legislation + successfully defended IN's near-total abortion ban (SB1) to IN Supreme Court.

4.note: Indiana Right to Life PAC endorsee, 100% pro-life rating; no abortion-industry funding found.

2.note: Issued CIDs to gender clinics; defended SEA 480 minor-gender-procedure ban; led 22-state coalition.

4.note: Parents' Bill of Rights includes pronoun-change notification + library-book removal guidance.

0.note: Parents' Bill of Rights Vol 3 dedicated to school choice/education freedom.

1.note: Pronoun-change parental-notification + health-record provisions in Parents' Bill of Rights.

2.note: Launched 'Eyes on Education' portal + curriculum-transparency provisions targeting CRT/gender ideology.

3.note: Sued ~50 porn sites under IN age-verification law; led 24-state SCOTUS brief supporting age verification.

0.note: Added religious-liberty section to Parents' Bill of Rights covering religious expression/prayer in schools.

3.note: Religious-liberty section protects prayer in public schools.

4.note: Led 17-AG FERC motion vs BlackRock + multistate antitrust suit vs BlackRock/State Street/Vanguard over ESG.

1.note: Won 7th Cir stay upholding SEA 10 voter-ID; sued DHS/USCIS for citizenship-verification, won settlement.

2.note: Sued Biden admin on immigration/asylum, DOL ESG rule, EPA — resisting federal overreach.

3.note: Litigation posture affirms state sovereignty against federal action.

2.note: Sued St. Joseph County + threatened multiple cities under Indiana's sanctuary ban.

1.note: NRA 'A' rating; backed firearm-transport legislation — opposes gun control (red-flag/AWB/registries).

kris-kobach.notes_set: Kansas AG (R), filed for re-election 2026-01-08 (GOP primary 2026-08-04); former KS Sec of State, architect of state immigration-enforcement law (AZ SB1070). Hardline-conservative record. Documented: KFL-endorsed pro-life; on-record opposing same-sex marriage; defends SB244 bathroom/birth-sex + SB63 minor-gender-care ban; warned districts vs concealing student gender transition; first KS porn age-verification enforcement suit; on record gay-couple adoption 'not good for kids'; led antitrust/ESG suits vs BlackRock/State Street/Vanguard; signature voter-ID/proof-of-citizenship + Crosscheck + Trump fraud-commission + won RNC v Virginia; 'Bidenbucks' suit; 51-sheriff endorsement + fentanyl/retail-crime crackdown; prolific anti-Biden federal-overreach suits; KBI-ICE 287(g), 14th-Amend birthright-citizenship denial, foreign-farmland ban; killed CCL fee (constitutional carry) + NRA endorsed + led 21-state ATF suit. DEVIATION NOTE: Gov. Kelly (D) sued him Oct 2025 alleging he selectively declines to litigate Trump-era federal overreach (partisan-asymmetry critique) — not a policy reversal. Null where no evidence (Christian-liberty cells, CBDC, balanced-budget, euthanasia/IVF, NFA/castle).

0.note: Self-described '100% pro-life,' Kansans for Life endorsee.

4.note: KFL-endorsed Republican; no abortion-industry PAC funding found.

0.note: On record opposing marriage equality, comparing it to polygamy.

1.note: On record opposing same-sex marriage in law.

2.note: Defends SB244 (birth-sex bathroom/ID) + SB63 minor-gender-care ban; appealing injunction.

4.note: Sent letters to all KS districts attacking policies concealing student gender transition.

1.note: 'Parents have a constitutional right to control the upbringing of their children' re gender interventions.

3.note: Filed first KS enforcement lawsuit under porn age-verification law.

4.note: On record gay couples adopting on equal terms is 'certainly not good for the kids.'
Led multistate antitrust suit vs BlackRock/State Street/Vanguard (coal); pressed NAAG on ESG.

1.note: Authored KS SAFE Act voter-ID + proof-of-citizenship law; Crosscheck; Trump fraud-commission vice-chair; won RNC v Virginia.

3.note: Prosecuted ballot signature-forgery; SAFE Act adds mail-ballot signature verification.

4.note: Joined Montana v Biden vs EO 14019 federal voter-registration ('Bidenbucks') mandate.

0.note: Endorsed by 51 KS sheriffs; 'back the blue,' ties crime to Democrat 'defund' push.

1.note: Fentanyl/organized-retail-crime crackdown + pushed heavier penalties (anti-soft-on-crime).
Led/joined multistate suits declining federal mandates (student-loan forgiveness, HHS transgender rule).

2.note: Resisted federal overreach on life/religion (HHS trans rule, won) and guns (ATF suits).

3.note: Prolific state-sovereignty litigation vs federal action.

4.note: Opposed federal capture of state elections (EO 14019 'Bidenbucks' suit).

1.note: Architect of AZ SB1070-style mandatory enforcement; signed KBI-ICE 287(g) deputizing agents for immigration arrests.

3.note: Mandates E-Verify for AG-office hires/contractors; pushed statewide E-Verify (blocked by business lobby).

4.note: On record 14th Amendment doesn't guarantee birthright citizenship; pushed foreign-farmland ban SB446.

0.note: Eliminated $100 CCL fee: 'Kansans should not have to pay the state a fee... to exercise a constitutional right.'

1.note: NRA-endorsed, 'A' rating — opposes gun control (red-flag/AWB/registries).

4.note: Led 21-state suit vs ATF 'engaged in the business' rule.

andy-beshear.notes_set: Kentucky Governor (D), re-elected 2023 (term to Dec 2027); former KY AG; churchgoing Disciples-of-Christ deacon (faith genuine but largely deployed toward progressive ends). Solidly progressive on life/marriage-gender/school-choice/guns/conscience. CONTRARY (HARD): vetoed Amendment 2 + HB3/HB90 abortion bills, campaigned to add exceptions, PP/NARAL-backed; vetoed SB150 (minor gender-care + pronoun/bathroom) + signed conversion-therapy ban EO; lead opponent of 2024 school-choice Amendment 2; COVID EOs barred in-person worship (enjoined by 6th Cir); vetoed photo-voter-ID SB2; declined to back state-sovereignty border resolution; called for red-flag law after Louisville shooting. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS (credited TRUE): genuinely pro-police — opposed 'defund,' funded body armor + restored police pensions, signed LE bills; deployed ~850 KY National Guard to the US-Mexico border under both Trump and Biden. Null where no evidence (IVF/euthanasia, economic-stewardship cells, most self-defense/federalism sub-items).

0.note: Vetoed/opposed 2022 Amendment 2 (no constitutional abortion right); said he'd vote no.

1.note: Vetoed HB3 (15-week ban) and HB90; campaigned to ADD abortion exceptions.

4.note: Backed by Planned Parenthood PAC and NARAL.

2.note: Vetoed SB150 banning minor gender procedures + school pronoun/bathroom rules (overridden).

4.note: Signed EO banning conversion therapy on minors; vetoed 2025 reversal bill.

0.note: Lead opponent of 2024 Amendment 2 school choice; 'public dollars belong only in public schools.'

1.note: Vetoed SB150, which included parental-notification on gender, opposing it as parental 'interference.'
2020 COVID EOs barred in-person worship; enjoined by 6th Circuit (no conscience exemption).

3.note: Banned in-person worship; state police logged congregants' plates at a drive-in Easter service.

1.note: Vetoed SB2 photo-voter-ID (overridden), citing voter-suppression concerns.

0.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: opposed 'defund,' funded body armor + restored police pensions, signed LE/public-safety bills.

3.note: Declined GOP state-sovereignty border resolution; warned the theory 'was used to support secession.'

0.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: deployed ~850 KY National Guard to the US-Mexico border under both Trump and Biden.

1.note: Called for a red-flag law after the 2023 Louisville shooting.

laura-kelly.notes_set: Kansas Governor (D), term-limited (second term ends Jan 2027, not running 2026); former state senator. Progressive veto record (much overridden by the GOP supermajority). CONTRARY (HARD): pro-abortion-rights, vetoed born-alive + Woman's-Right-to-Know + abortion-coercion bills, opposed Value Them Both, PP + EMILY's-List endorsee; vetoed SB180 'Women's Bill of Rights,' trans-athlete ban, minor gender-care bans (2024/2025), bathroom bill (2026); vetoed school-choice tax-credit (HB2468) + SB24 + Parents' Bill of Rights; vetoed SAVE Kansas Act citizenship-check + SB4 mail-ballot grace-period repeal; declined to send KS Guard to Texas border + vetoed $15.7M border earmark; vetoed SB446 foreign-farmland ban; vetoed concealed-carry-age bill + supports universal background checks/AWB. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION (credited TRUE): documented pro-police funding (+$100M state public-safety spending, KHP raises, 'historic investment in law enforcement'). Other mild deviations noted but not scored: signed $1.2B bipartisan tax cut after vetoing the larger flat-tax; let porn age-verification (SB394) become law without veto. Null where no evidence (Christian-liberty, CBDC/usury, euthanasia/IVF).

0.note: Pro-abortion-rights; vetoed 'born alive' bill; opposed Value Them Both amendment.

1.note: Vetoed Woman's-Right-to-Know + abortion-coercion bills.

4.note: Endorsed by EMILY's List and Planned Parenthood.

2.note: Vetoed SB180 'Women's Bill of Rights,' trans-athlete ban, minor gender-care bans (2024/2025), bathroom bill (2026).

0.note: 'Never been a proponent of vouchers'; vetoed school-choice tax-credit (HB2468) and SB24.

1.note: Vetoed the Parents' Bill of Rights (HB2236) multiple years.
Vetoed the SAVE Kansas Act voter-roll/citizenship-check bill (overridden).

3.note: Vetoed SB4 ending the mail-ballot grace period (protecting mail-in ballots).

0.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: +$100M state public-safety funding, KHP pay raises, 'historic investment in law enforcement.'
Declined to send KS National Guard to the Texas border; vetoed the $15.7M border-deployment earmark.

4.note: Vetoed SB446 restricting foreign (adversary) farmland ownership as 'overly broad.'

1.note: Vetoed concealed-carry-age bill; supports universal background checks + assault-weapons ban.

david-toland.notes_set: CANDIDACY CORRECTED (high-value currency fix): Toland announced 2025-07-22 he will NOT run for Governor 'or any other elected office, in 2026' — prior record's 2026-08-04 primary candidacy was stale. He is the sitting KS Lt. Governor + Secretary of Commerce (both appointments under Gov. Kelly), completing his term through Jan 2027. Record is an appointed economic-development technocrat with essentially no public social/cultural positions — 48 of 49 cells null (no evidence; not inferred from party). Only documented position: as architect/lead negotiator of KS's APEX program he secured ~$829M (Panasonic) + ~$304M (Integra) state-incentive packages — championing large corporate-government subsidy partnerships, contrary to the anti-corporate-capture economic-stewardship plank.

4.note: Architect of APEX: ~$829M Panasonic + ~$304M Integra state-incentive packages — champions large corporate-government subsidy partnerships.
